_notes: Two descriptors: one strength, one flaw. Phrases rather than single words — 'fiercely loyal' reads better than 'loyal'. Aim for specificity that implies backstory.
strength: fiercely loyal to those they've decided to call friend
quietly perceptive — notices what others miss and says nothing until it matters
steady under pressure, almost always the last one to panic
generous with time and resources in a way that feels genuine rather than performed
quick-witted and adaptable when everything goes wrong
honest even when honesty costs something
patient in a way that borders on the unnerving
protective of people who can't protect themselves
curious about everything, endlessly and without embarrassment
calm in the presence of things that would break most people
principled in a way that isn't performative — they simply hold the line
resourceful when all the obvious options are gone
warm with strangers in a way that disarms before suspicion can form
relentless in pursuit of what they believe is right, regardless of cost
deeply empathetic — feels what others feel, sometimes inconveniently
precise and methodical under conditions that demand it
brave in the quiet, unremarkable way that actually counts
honest about their own limitations, which is rarer than it sounds
deliberate and unhurried — never acts before thinking, even under pressure
intensely focused on what they care about, to the exclusion of almost everything else
reliable in a way that people stop noticing until it's gone
self-sufficient — rarely asks for help, rarely needs to
perceptive about people's motivations in a way they keep to themselves
steady in their convictions without being rigid about them
flaw: holds grudges long after everyone else has moved on, and remembers every detail
arrogant about their own judgment in ways that create dangerous blind spots
reckless when the stakes feel high enough to justify it
secretive by reflex — withholds information even when it helps no one
slow to trust and quick to revoke it, sometimes without clear cause
always calculating what they stand to gain, even in moments that don't require it
cruel in small, almost imperceptible ways that they don't acknowledge
cowardly precisely when it would matter most
unable to let go of the past, which crowds the present
dishonest by habit, even in situations where there's nothing to gain
too proud to ask for help they badly need
impulsive — decisions made before consequences are considered
cold with people, treating them as means to ends without meaning to
bitter about something that happened a long time ago and still shapes everything
envious of what others have, and constantly aware of the gap
stubborn to the point of self-destruction when challenged
manipulative in ways they don't fully acknowledge even to themselves
deeply self-doubting beneath a surface that shows none of it
prone to cruelty when frustrated or cornered
unable to admit when they're wrong — will reconstruct events to avoid it
dismissive of people they've decided don't matter
compulsively self-reliant in situations that require cooperation
so focused on long-term goals that people in front of them become invisible
quick to anger in private, slow to show it publicly, and the pressure builds
